Title: ICE1 and ZOU determine the depth of primary seed dormancy in Arabidopsis independently of their role in endosperm development

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in endosperm of developing seeds; also in stomata of leaves
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in endosperm of developing seeds; also in stomata of leaves
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed maturation temperature (16°C versus 22°C); cold stratification; exogenous potassium nitrate; exogenous GA3; after-ripening
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Seed developmental program; not required for low-temperature-induced dormancy increase during seed maturation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses ABI3 transcription by binding the ABI3 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: ABA-associated seed dormancy establishment in the endosperm
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms homo- and heterodimers with ZOU
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Represses germination and promotes primary seed dormancy
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of ICE1 increases primary seed dormancy and elevates seed ABA levels
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Direct endosperm-expressed repressor of ABI3 that is necessary for normal ABA-associated dormancy depth
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Loss-of-function mutant analysis (ice1-2, ice1-3); complementation with pICE1:ICE1-GFP; double mutants with aba2-1 and dog1-2; ABA quantification; seed coat bedding assay; qPCR; ChIP-qPCR on endosperm-enriched fractions
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ice1 mutants showed reduced germination of fresh seeds, indicating increased primary dormancy. 2. The dormancy phenotype was complemented by pICE1:ICE1-GFP, showing ICE1 is required for normal dormancy control. 3. ice1 dormancy depended on ABA biosynthesis and DOG1, because aba2-1 and dog1-2 suppressed the dormancy phenotype without rescuing seed morphology. 4. ice1 seeds contained increased ABA, and ice1 endosperm more strongly inhibited embryo greening, indicating elevated endospermic ABA activity. 5. ICE1 directly bound the ABI3 promoter and loss of ICE1 increased ABI3 transcript, supporting repression of ABI3 by ICE1.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of ICE1 causes aberrant endosperm consumption, darker shrivelled seeds, and altered embryo morphology, although these morphological defects are genetically separable from dormancy
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ICE1 effect on dormancy is zygotic/endosperm based rather than maternally inherited. ICE1 was not required for the increase in dormancy caused by low seed maturation temperature. No ChIP enrichment was detected at promoters of NCED6, NCED9, CYP707A1, CYP707A2, or ABI5 under the tested conditions.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in endosperm during seed development
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in endosperm during seed development
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed maturation temperature (16°C versus 22°C); cold stratification; exogenous potassium nitrate; exogenous GA3; after-ripening
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Seed developmental program; not required for low-temperature-induced dormancy increase during seed maturation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Acts with ICE1 as an inhibitor of the seed maturation program in endosperm; direct target regulation for ABI3 was not experimentally shown here for ZOU alone
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: ABA-associated seed dormancy establishment in the endosperm
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms heterodimers and homodimers with ICE1
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Represses germination and promotes primary seed dormancy
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of ZOU increases primary seed dormancy and elevates seed ABA levels
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Endosperm-expressed regulator contributing to normal limitation of dormancy depth; additive with ICE1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Loss-of-function mutant analysis (zou-3, zou-4); double mutant analysis with ice1-2; dormancy-breaking treatments; ABA quantification
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. zou mutants showed reduced germination of fresh seeds, indicating increased primary dormancy. 2. Cold stratification, GA3 and after-ripening rescued the dormancy phenotype. 3. The ice1 zou double mutant had an additive increase in dormancy, consistent with partially independent contributions. 4. zou mutant seeds accumulated more ABA than the wild type. 5. ZOU acts in the endosperm and contributes to dormancy control independently of its known role in endosperm development.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of ZOU causes defective endosperm consumption and abnormal seed morphology in addition to increased dormancy
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct binding of ZOU to dormancy-related targets was not tested in this study. The paper emphasizes additive dormancy effects with ice1 and increased ABA in mature seeds.
